context: Tests whether the agent corrects multiple anti-patterns present in the original Dockerfile: splitting RUN layers, using ADD instead of COPY, running as root, floating base tags, and not cleaning apt caches. The rewritten Dockerfile should combine apt commands, use COPY, and clean up in the same layer.

checklist.name: RUN commands combined
checklist.description: apt-get update, apt-get install, and cache cleanup (rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*) are chained in a single RUN instruction using &&, rather than separate RUN instructions

checklist.name: Cache cleaned in same layer
checklist.description: The apt cache cleanup (rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* or equivalent) is inside the SAME RUN instruction as apt-get install, not a separate RUN

checklist.name: COPY used not ADD
checklist.description: Dockerfile uses COPY for all file copy operations; ADD is NOT present for plain file/directory copying

checklist.name: Pinned base image tag
checklist.description: FROM uses a specific Python version tag (e.g., python:3.11-slim) and NOT :latest

checklist.name: Minimal base image
checklist.description: Final image uses python:X.X-slim, python:X.X-alpine, or distroless rather than the full python:X.X image

checklist.name: Non-root user
checklist.description: A non-root user is created and USER instruction is set before CMD/ENTRYPOINT

checklist.name: Dependency files copied first
checklist.description: COPY of requirements.txt appears BEFORE the pip install RUN instruction

checklist.name: Exec-form CMD
checklist.description: CMD uses JSON array syntax rather than bare shell string form

checklist.name: .dockerignore with Python entries
checklist.description: .dockerignore is present and contains at least one Python-specific entry such as __pycache__/, *.pyc, or .venv/
